summary: Shipped the `semantic` feature (semantic search + embed + similar subcommands, powered by fastembed + ONNX Runtime) in default builds. Three fixes bundled: (1) fixed the clap `--lang` panic in `tldr semantic` by renaming the subcommand-local `lang: Option<Vec<String>>` field to `langs` with `#[arg(long = "langs", value_delimiter = ',')]`, resolving the TypeId mismatch against the global `--lang: Option<Language>` arg. The SAME bug existed on `tldr embed` — fixed preemptively in the same commit. (2) gated `crates/tldr-core/tests/semantic_tests.rs` AND the second test file `crates/tldr-core/tests/semantic_test.rs` with `#![cfg(feature = "semantic")]` so workspace test builds without the feature flag compile cleanly. (3) flipped `default = []` → `default = ["semantic"]` in both `crates/tldr-core/Cargo.toml` and `crates/tldr-cli/Cargo.toml`. Added a regression test covering all three clap entry points. End-to-end verified: plain `cargo build --release` ships a 65MB binary with `semantic`, `embed`, `similar` subcommands; `tldr semantic "walker that skips node_modules" /tmp/fixture` returns the expected function at score 0.89 after a ~110MB model download on first run.

panic_repro.output_before_fix: thread 'main' panicked at clap_builder-4.5.56/src/parser/error.rs:32:9: Mismatch between definition and access of `lang`. Could not downcast to TypeId(0x8f24b19dbf5d8456e45d273df5ffe406), need to downcast to TypeId(0xc895fb3e4b2b14a22d76171e867603bb)
panic_repro.root_cause: Global `--lang` arg on top-level Cli struct is `Option<Language>` (single-valued enum). `SemanticArgs` and `EmbedArgs` each defined `pub lang: Option<Vec<String>>`. Clap globbed both under the same `lang` key and panicked at runtime on TypeId downcast whenever either subcommand saw `--lang <value>`.
panic_repro.output_after_fix: JSON result with total_chunks: 1, top-ranked function walker_that_skips_node_modules at score 0.58 (or filtered out when extension doesn't match — non-panic path).

fix_1_lang_panic.before: pub lang: Option<Vec<String>> (no explicit long=, inherits field name)
fix_1_lang_panic.reason_for_option_a_over_b: Option B (change the type to Option<Language>) would lose the multi-language filter capability. Option A preserves multi-value semantics and eliminates the TypeId collision at the same time.
fix_1_lang_panic.collateral_embed_fix: embed.rs had the identical bug shape (pub lang: Option<Vec<String>>). Fixed it in the same commit — otherwise `tldr embed --lang rust` would continue to panic after `tldr semantic` was fixed, because embed also ships by default now.
fix_1_lang_panic.value_delimiter_rationale: The pre-existing doc comment promised `--lang rust,python` comma-separated syntax, but clap's default does not split on commas. Added value_delimiter = ',' to make the documented syntax actually work.
fix_1_lang_panic.docstring_correction: The pre-VAL-009 doc comment said `e.g., rust, python` — but `crate::Language::from_extension("rust")` returns None (it expects `rs`, not `rust`). Corrected the doc to describe extensions explicitly and point users at the global `--lang <LANG>` for name-based selection.

usage_examples.new_global_flag: tldr semantic 'q' path --lang rust (global single-language selection — works via Cli.lang)

fix_2_test_gating.change: Added `#![cfg(feature = "semantic")]` as line 1 of each file.
fix_2_test_gating.two_files: The task spec named the plural form `semantic_tests.rs` but the singular `semantic_test.rs` (a separate TDD red-phase file from Session 16) had the identical `use tldr_core::semantic::...` imports and the identical failure mode. Gated both to ensure `cargo test --workspace` without the feature compiles in clean standalone.
fix_2_test_gating.preserved_behavior: With --features semantic both files compile and their tests run as before; without the feature they are excluded from the test build.

downstream_impact.plain_cargo_build_release: now compiles the semantic stack (fastembed + ort + tokenizers + hf-hub)
downstream_impact.cargo_install_tldr_code: ships a semantic-enabled binary by default
downstream_impact.binary_size_increase: on mac arm64: 45MB (no-semantic release) → 65MB (semantic release). +20MB from ONNX Runtime + fastembed.
downstream_impact.cold_build_time_increase: first build of the semantic deps takes ~2-3min on a warm cargo cache (ort + fastembed + tokenizers transitive graph). Subsequent builds are near-instant after incremental compilation caches.
downstream_impact.first_run_model_download: ~110MB from Hugging Face hub for the default arctic-m model; cached under ~/.cache/tldr (or OS equivalent via dirs crate).

coverage.test_semantic_langs_flag_does_not_panic: asserts `tldr semantic q <dir> --langs rs` exits without a clap panic or TypeId error
coverage.test_semantic_global_lang_flag_does_not_: asserts `tldr semantic q <dir> --lang rust` (global flag inherited from Cli struct) exits without a clap panic or TypeId error
coverage.test_embed_langs_flag_does_not_panic: asserts `tldr embed <dir> --langs rs` exits without a clap panic or TypeId error — protects against regression on the embed command's parallel fix

verification.cargo_build_release_default_features: success (binary ships semantic, embed, similar subcommands)
verification.cargo_test_tldr_core_lib: 4737 passed / 0 failed / 356 ignored (up from VAL-008 baseline 4675 — the +62 comes from the now-compiled semantic module's unit tests that were previously gated out with `default = []`)
verification.cargo_test_workspace_no_features_compile: compiles; only pre-existing gate2_corpus_scan + gate3_octagon_ab_comparison test binaries fail to compile (they reference `ENABLE_GUARD_NARROWING` / `ENABLE_OCTAGON_DOMAIN` which do not exist at HEAD — this failure also exists pre-VAL-009 and is unrelated to this milestone)
verification.cargo_test_workspace_semantic_features_c: same two pre-existing gate failures — no new failures introduced by feature flip
verification.installed_binary_help_shows_three_subcom: embed, semantic, similar all listed under Commands
verification.lang_flag_no_panic: tldr semantic 'q' path --langs rs exits 0 with valid JSON; tldr semantic 'q' path --lang rust exits 0 with valid JSON (global flag path); tldr embed path --langs rs exits 0 with valid JSON

files_deliberately_NOT_modified: .github/workflows/release.yml (CI/packaging story is VAL-010 scope, not VAL-009)
crates/tldr-core/tests/gate2_corpus_scan.rs (pre-existing broken test; references ENABLE_GUARD_NARROWING that does not exist at HEAD)
crates/tldr-core/tests/gate3_octagon_ab_comparison.rs (pre-existing broken test; references ENABLE_OCTAGON_DOMAIN that does not exist at HEAD)
crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/references.rs (separate `pub lang: Option<String>` — different type, different subcommand; no reported panic; not in VAL-009 scope)

constraints_observed.no_github_push: Local commit only. User decides release tagging separately.
constraints_observed.no_release_workflow_changes: .github/workflows/release.yml untouched. linux-aarch64 or any other platform concerns are a separate VAL-010.
constraints_observed.no_weakened_assertions: Regression test asserts on panic-free exit AND absence of TypeId mismatch in stderr — both load-bearing.
constraints_observed.no_git_stash: Used `git show HEAD:<path>` to confirm pre-existing state of gate2/gate3 broken tests without touching working tree.
constraints_observed.explicit_git_add: Commit staged via explicit `git add <path>` for each of the 9 files. ~90 files of stale cargo-fmt drift in the working tree were NOT captured.

discoveries: The `embed` subcommand had the identical `pub lang: Option<Vec<String>>` shape as `semantic` — same TypeId-mismatch bug. The task spec named only `semantic.rs`, but fixing only semantic would leave embed panicking once semantic shipped by default. Both commands ship in the same default release, so both needed to be fixed atomically.

A second test file `crates/tldr-core/tests/semantic_test.rs` (singular) exists alongside the plural `semantic_tests.rs` and has the identical unconditional `use tldr_core::semantic::...` imports. The task spec named only the plural form, but gating only one would leave the other failing to compile when the feature is off. Both gated.

The pre-VAL-009 doc comment on `SemanticArgs.lang` promised `e.g., rust, python` but the implementation (index.rs:222) calls `crate::Language::from_extension(s)` which rejects full language names. The renamed `langs` field got an accurate docstring plus a working `value_delimiter = ','` for the documented comma-separated syntax.

`cargo test -p tldr-core --lib` jumped from 4675 (VAL-008 baseline) to 4737 with the feature flipped on — the +62 tests come from the semantic module's own #[test]-annotated lib tests which previously got gated out by `default = []`. Those tests are now part of the default lib test suite.

remaining_work_and_followups.val_010_candidate: The repo's .github/workflows/release.yml assumes a no-semantic build target. With defaults flipped, CI release builds now need to cross-compile fastembed + ort for all target triples. This is not a VAL-009 concern — it's a release-engineering concern the user asked to defer.
remaining_work_and_followups.gate2_gate3_broken_tests: Two tldr-core integration tests reference constants (`ENABLE_GUARD_NARROWING`, `ENABLE_OCTAGON_DOMAIN`) that are not defined in `crates/tldr-core/src/dataflow/abstract_interp.rs`. These were broken at the Initial release commit. Not addressed in VAL-009 per spec scope.
remaining_work_and_followups.ci_model_download: First-run semantic tests download ~110MB from HF hub. A separate follow-up may want to pin model artifacts into CI cache, mock the fastembed layer in tests, or mark network-dependent tests `#[ignore]` so they only run in targeted e2e pipelines.
